description: Global (self-hosted admin) config for the central Renovate runner — NOT this repo's own
dependency config (that stays in .github/renovate.json). Loaded by .github/workflows/
renovate.yml; see its header comment for the full auth/token-scope rationale.
customEnvVariables.BASE_REF arms the supply-chain tamper gate in the binary-checksum
postUpgradeTask fleet-wide: scripts/refresh-binary-checksums.sh only activates its gate
when BASE_REF is set, so it then re-pins a *_SHA256 only when the adjacent *_VERSION
actually changed vs BASE_REF and FAILS if a SHA changed under an unchanged version (a
swapped release asset on a fixed tag). origin/HEAD is base-branch-agnostic — it resolves
to each managed repo's default branch in Renovate's working clone, so `git show
origin/HEAD:<file>` reads the pre-upgrade version regardless of main/master. Renovate
injects customEnvVariables into the postUpgradeTask child env directly (no shell executor
needed); it is NOT in allowedEnv on purpose, so a managed repo's own `env` can't override
BASE_REF and disarm its gate.
Dedup the security-remediation seam (repo-management#144): Dependabot is the fleet's
security *floor* (fast, event-driven, enabled via repo-management's security config), so
Renovate — the freshness *ceiling* — must NOT also open vulnerability-remediation PRs, or
a single advisory yields two competing PRs. Disabling it here leaves Renovate purely
proactive; the security path is Dependabot's alone.
Fleet-wide automerge for minor updates (majors and patches have their own rules
below); a repo's own renovate.json packageRules can override. platformAutomerge is
OFF deliberately: the runner authenticates as the CI App, which holds bypass_mode:
always on the fleet's required-checks ruleset, so GitHub-native auto-merge would
treat its PRs as immediately mergeable and the ruleset gate could not be trusted.
With platform automerge off, Renovate itself merges only after it observes the
branch checks green (its default; no ignoreTests here), independent of bypass
rights. Repos without a CI workflow yet simply never satisfy that, so their PRs
wait (fail closed) until they adopt the scaffold. Pre-1.0 (0.x) minors automerge
too — deliberately rejecting Renovate's recommended '!/^0/' exclusion of pre-1.0
versions as conventionally-breaking: this fleet's merge gate is CI, not the semver
position. A 0.x minor that actually breaks a consumer fails that consumer's CI and
never merges; one that passes is safe to take. (0.x patch and major already ride
their own rules with no such exclusion, so this just makes the minor case match.)
Fleet-wide automerge for MAJOR updates too — a deliberate, risk-accepted policy: the
maintainer wants majors to land automatically once CI proves them green, trading the
human pre-merge gate for the same CI gate every other update type rides. It is safe
only because the merge is genuinely CI-gated: platformAutomerge stays OFF (see the
minor rule), so Renovate merges a major PR only after it observes the branch checks
green, and a repo with no CI workflow never satisfies that (fail closed). The 3-day
minimumReleaseAge still applies as a community-vetting window; the two sensitive
release-privilege actions below override it to 14 days, majors included, since their
packageNames rule is ordered after this one and only widens the wait.
A repo's own renovate.json packageRules can still override this to restore a manual
major gate where a given repo wants one.
Both actions are handed the fleet App's private key at runtime — create-github-
app-token mints the installation token from it, release-please-action drives the
release/tag-cutting flow with it — and the App is a ruleset bypass actor, so a
compromised release reaching this fleet inherits real privilege. A longer
minimumReleaseAge gives the community more time to flag a bad release before it's
eligible; automerge itself stays on (inherited from the rules above, not reset
here) since the wait is the actual mitigation, not a manual-review gate.
